mapped.summary: Mapped models + runtime operations for ACE-Step 1.5 at /Users/zathras/WAVESMITH_Project/engine/ACE-Step-1.5 (source-verified, read-only). Key findings: (a) Default DiT is acestep-v15-turbo, bundled in the unified HF repo ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5 together with vae, Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B, and the default LM acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B; optional sub-repos cover base/sft/turbo-shift variants, XL (4B) DiTs, and 0.6B/4B LMs. Capability gating in the API is binary via config.json is_turbo: turbo models report [text2music, repaint, cover, cover-nofsq]; everything else reports the full 7-task list — but README + Gradio UI gate Extract/Lego/Complete to PURE base models only (sft excluded), so the API's supported_task_types over-reports for sft. (b) With default flags NOTHING downloads at server start: ACESTEP_NO_INIT defaults to true (lazy). The unified repo (~10 GB; bf16 weights ≈ DiT 4.7 + LM-1.7B 3.4 + TE 1.2 + VAE 0.33 GB) snapshots into <repo>/checkpoints/ on the first generation request or POST /v1/init, from HuggingFace (auto-fallback ModelScope based on a Google-reachability probe). No download-progress endpoint exists; progress is stderr/tqdm only, with the last log line surfaced as progress_text in /query_result responses. Critically, lazy init runs synchronously on the asyncio event loop, so /health stalls during the entire download+load. (c) /health (no auth) returns models_initialized/llm_initialized/loaded_model; it does not distinguish 'downloading' from 'not loaded'. (d) acestep-download (entry point acestep.model_downloader:main) pre-fetches: bare invocation downloads the main unified repo; --model/--all/--dir/--force/--token/--list supported. (e) On a 32 GB M2 Max: MPS detection reports ~75% of unified memory (~24 GB → tier6b borderline); MPS overrides force lm_backend=mlx (vllm impossible), no quantization, no torch.compile (redirected to mx.compile), no CPU offload; DiT runs via native MLX decoder (default on for mps/cpu), PyTorch dtype float32 on MPS, MLX VAE chunk auto-tuned (512 for ≤36 GB, override ACESTEP_MLX_VAE_CHUNK); LM loads via mlx-lm. DiT+1.7B LM fits comfortably in 32 GB unified memory. (f) Full .env.example list and exact api_server CLI flags (--host/--port/--api-key/--download-source/--init-llm/--lm-model-path/--no-init) documented with defaults; macOS-relevant extras (ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND=mlx, ACESTEP_MLX_VAE_CHUNK, ACESTEP_MLX_VAE_FP16, ACESTEP_MPS_DECODE_OFFLOAD) identified. (g) The update check lives in the launcher shell scripts (CHECK_UPDATE="true" hardcoded in start_api_server_macos.sh) and PROMPTS interactively when remote differs — for unattended runs bypass the launcher (run `uv run acestep-api` directly; the Python server has no update check) or edit the script to CHECK_UPDATE="false".

claims.topic: (a) Model registry: unified main repo + sub-model repos
claims.claim: The main HuggingFace repo is ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5 and contains exactly 4 components downloaded as one snapshot: acestep-v15-turbo (default DiT), vae, Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B (text encoder), acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B (default LM). Separately downloadable sub-repos: LMs acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B and acestep-5Hz-lm-4B; DiTs acestep-v15-turbo-shift3, acestep-v15-sft, acestep-v15-base, acestep-v15-turbo-shift1, acestep-v15-turbo-continuous; XL 4B DiTs acestep-v15-xl-base, acestep-v15-xl-sft, acestep-v15-xl-turbo (all under the ACE-Step/ HF org, same ids on ModelScope). One community VAE: scragvae -> scragnog/Ace-Step-1.5-ScragVAE.

evidence.quote: MAIN_MODEL_REPO = "ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5" ... SUBMODEL_REGISTRY: Dict[str, str] = { "acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B": "ACE-Step/acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B", ... "acestep-v15-xl-turbo": "ACE-Step/acestep-v15-xl-turbo", } ... MAIN_MODEL_COMPONENTS = ["acestep-v15-turbo", "vae", "Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B", "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B"] ... DEFAULT_LM_MODEL = "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B" ... VAE_REGISTRY ... "scragvae": "scragnog/Ace-Step-1.5-ScragVAE"

MODEL_REPO_MAPPING = { "acestep-v15-turbo": "ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5", "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B": "ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5", "vae": "ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5", "Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B": "ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5", ... } DEFAULT_REPO_ID = "ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5"

claims.claim: Default DiT is acestep-v15-turbo (ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH default, hardcoded fallback in both server and handler). Approximate bf16 weight sizes from the repo's own calibration constants: 2B DiT (turbo/base/sft) ~4.7 GB; XL 4B DiT ~9.0 GB; VAE ~0.33 GB; Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B text encoder ~1.2 GB; LM weights 0.6B=1.2 GB, 1.7B=3.4 GB, 4B=8.0 GB. Docs state ~10 GB disk for core models, consistent with the 4-component main repo (~9.6 GB of weights).

evidence.quote: app.state._config_path = os.getenv("ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH", "acestep-v15-turbo")

MODEL_VRAM = { "dit_turbo": 4.7, "dit_base": 4.7, "dit_xl_turbo": 9.0, "dit_xl_base": 9.0, "vae": 0.33, "text_encoder": 1.2, ... } LM_VRAM = { "0.6B": {"weights": 1.2, ...}, "1.7B": {"weights": 3.4, ...}, "4B": {"weights": 8.0, ...} }
| Disk | ~10GB for core models |
if config_path is None: config_path = "acestep-v15-turbo"

claims.topic: (a) Capability matrix — task types per variant
claims.claim: Task constants: TASK_TYPES_TURBO = [text2music, repaint, cover, cover-nofsq]; TASK_TYPES_BASE adds extract, lego, complete (7 total). The API's GET /v1/models supported_task_types is decided ONLY by the is_turbo boolean in each checkpoint's config.json (turbo -> 4 tasks, non-turbo or missing config -> all 7). However the Gradio UI and README gate Extract/Lego/Complete more strictly to PURE base models (path contains 'base' but not 'sft'/'turbo'): README matrix says acestep-v15-base and acestep-v15-xl-base support Extract/Lego/Complete with CFG and 50 steps, while sft/xl-sft (CFG, 50 steps) and turbo/xl-turbo (no CFG, 8 steps) support only Text2Music/Cover/Repaint. So treat the API's 7-task report for sft models as over-broad; ground truth for capabilities is: turbo & sft -> text2music/cover/repaint(+cover-nofsq); pure base only -> + extract/lego/complete.

evidence.quote: TASK_TYPES_TURBO = ["text2music", "repaint", "cover", "cover-nofsq"] ... TASK_TYPES_BASE = ["text2music", "repaint", "cover", "cover-nofsq", "extract", "lego", "complete"]
config_path = os.path.join(checkpoint_dir, model_name, "config.json") ... is_turbo = config.get("is_turbo", False); if is_turbo: return list(TASK_TYPES_TURBO); return list(TASK_TYPES_BASE)
is_pure_base ... path contains "base" and excludes "sft" and "turbo" ... if is_pure_base: task_choices = TASK_TYPES_BASE ... else: task_choices = TASK_TYPES_TURBO
| acestep-v15-base | ... Extract ✅ Lego ✅ Complete ✅ | acestep-v15-sft | ... Extract ❌ Lego ❌ Complete ❌ | acestep-v15-turbo | ... ❌ ❌ ❌ ... same pattern for xl-base ✅✅✅ / xl-sft ❌❌❌ / xl-turbo ❌❌❌

claims.topic: (b) What downloads on FIRST server start (default flags)
claims.claim: With default flags, NOTHING downloads at startup: env_bool("ACESTEP_NO_INIT", True) means lazy mode is the default; the server prints 'Models will be lazy-loaded on first request' and accepts requests immediately. Model download+load happens on the first generation job (or POST /v1/init, or eager mode via ACESTEP_NO_INIT=false). When init runs, ensure_model_downloaded() snapshots the ENTIRE unified repo ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5 into <project_root>/checkpoints (project_root = repo root, derived from the api_server.py file location), then the VAE/LM checks find their folders already present. Source selection: ACESTEP_DOWNLOAD_SOURCE env (huggingface/modelscope) else auto-detect by attempting a TCP connect to www.google.com:443 (reachable -> HuggingFace, else ModelScope), with automatic fallback to the other source on failure. Rough total: ~10 GB for the main repo (DiT 2B + VAE + text encoder + LM 1.7B). If ACESTEP_INIT_LLM resolves to enabled (default 'auto' -> tier decision; true on a 32GB Mac), the LM is also ensured (1.7B is already in the main repo).

evidence.quote: no_init = env_bool("ACESTEP_NO_INIT", True) ... if no_init: print("[API Server] Models will be lazy-loaded on first request") ... return
checkpoint_dir = os.path.join(project_root, "checkpoints") ... ensure_model_downloaded(dit_model_name, checkpoint_dir) ... ensure_model_downloaded("vae", checkpoint_dir)

is_unified_repo = repo_id == DEFAULT_REPO_ID ... download_dir = local_dir ... snapshot_download(repo_id=repo_id, local_dir=download_dir, local_dir_use_symlinks=False) ... prefer_source = os.environ.get("ACESTEP_DOWNLOAD_SOURCE", "").lower() ... use_huggingface = can_access_google()
def _get_project_root() -> str: current_file = os.path.abspath(__file__); return os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(current_file))

claims.claim: There is NO dedicated download-progress API endpoint. The full route list contains only /health, /v1/stats, /v1/models, /v1/model_inventory, /v1/init, /v1/reinitialize, /release_task, /query_result, /v1/audio, sample/format, LoRA, training, and OpenRouter-compat routes. The only API-visible signal is /query_result's progress_text field, which returns the last captured log line (a loguru sink plus a sys.stderr proxy feed a single-line LogBuffer; huggingface_hub tqdm bars write to stderr so download progress lines do land there) for tasks whose status is queued/running. Otherwise progress is only in process logs. Additionally, lazy init is a synchronous call executed inside an async function on the event loop (do_model_initialization called directly, not via executor), so during the first-request download+load the event loop blocks and even /health will not respond until init completes — a TypeScript client must use long HTTP timeouts or pre-warm via /v1/init (which DOES run in a thread executor) or pre-download via CLI.

evidence.quote: print("[API Server] First request received — lazy-loading models...") ... do_model_initialization(app=_AppProxy(app_state), **init_kwargs)  # direct sync call inside `async def ensure_models_initialized`
class LogBuffer: ... self.last_message = "Waiting" ... class StderrLogger: ... writes to terminal stderr and update in-memory buffer ... install_log_capture(...)
"progress_text": get_log_last_message() ... current_log = get_log_last_message() if status_int == 0 else record.progress_text
@app.post("/v1/init") ... result = await loop.run_in_executor(app.state.executor, lambda: initialize_models_for_request(...))

claims.claim: GET /health requires no API key and returns wrapped JSON {data: {status: "ok", service: "ACE-Step API", version: "1.0", models_initialized: bool(app.state._initialized), llm_initialized, loaded_model, loaded_lm_model}, code: 200, ...}. status is ALWAYS "ok" once the HTTP server is up — readiness must be inferred from models_initialized (false while lazy/not yet loaded, true after DiT load succeeds) and llm_initialized. There is no distinct 'downloading' state; during a lazy first-request init the event loop is blocked so /health simply hangs. If init fails, app.state._init_error is set and every subsequent generation request raises that error (jobs fail) while models_initialized stays false; the error string is only visible via failed job results, not /health. Eager loading (ACESTEP_NO_INIT=false) performs download+load inside the FastAPI lifespan before the server starts accepting connections, and raises RuntimeError (killing startup) if the primary DiT fails. /v1/models and /v1/model_inventory (API-key protected) report per-model {name, is_default, is_loaded, supported_task_types} by scanning the checkpoints dir, plus lm_models and llm_initialized.

evidence.quote: @app.get("/health") ... return wrap_response({"status": "ok", ... "models_initialized": bool(getattr(app.state, "_initialized", False)), "llm_initialized": ..., "loaded_model": ..., "loaded_lm_model": ...})
if getattr(app_state, "_initialized", False): return ... if getattr(app_state, "_init_error", None): raise RuntimeError(app_state._init_error)

if not ok: app.state._init_error = status_msg ... raise RuntimeError(status_msg)
    app.state._initialized = True
models = [{"name": name, "is_default": ..., "is_loaded": name in loaded_dit_models, "supported_task_types": _read_model_supported_tasks(...)} ...]

claims.claim: Console script acestep-download = acestep.model_downloader:main (pyproject [project.scripts]). To pre-fetch the default models run with no arguments: `uv run acestep-download` (or `acestep-download` in the venv) from the repo root — it downloads the main unified repo (vae, text encoder, acestep-v15-turbo DiT, LM 1.7B) into ./checkpoints (cwd-based: get_project_root() = ACESTEP_PROJECT_ROOT env or os.getcwd(); checkpoints dir overridable via --dir/-d or ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR). Flags: --model/-m NAME ('main' or a SUBMODEL_REGISTRY key; sub-model downloads pull main first unless --skip-main), --all/-a, --list/-l, --dir/-d PATH, --force/-f, --token/-t HF_TOKEN, --skip-main. Equivalent manual command documented in its epilog: `huggingface-cli download ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5 --local-dir ./checkpoints`. Skip-if-exists check: a component counts as present if its dir contains any known weights file (model.safetensors, diffusion_pytorch_model.safetensors, *.index.json, etc.).

evidence.quote: [project.scripts] acestep = "acestep.acestep_v15_pipeline:main"; acestep-api = "acestep.api_server:main"; acestep-download = "acestep.model_downloader:main"; acestep-openrouter = ...

acestep-download                          # Download main model (includes LM 1.7B) ... --model/-m, --all/-a, --list/-l, --dir/-d, --force/-f, --token/-t, --skip-main ... # Default: download main model (includes default LM 1.7B)

env_root = os.environ.get("ACESTEP_PROJECT_ROOT") ... return Path(os.getcwd()) ... env_dir = os.environ.get("ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR") ... return get_project_root() / "checkpoints" ... weight_filenames = ("model.safetensors", ...)

claims.topic: (e) Apple Silicon (32GB M2 Max) memory/config guidance
claims.claim: MPS detection: memory = torch.mps.recommended_max_memory() or fallback hw.memsize*0.75 (32 GB -> ~24 GB), so an M2 Max 32GB lands in tier6b (20-24 GB; recommended LM 1.7B, batch up to 8, durations up to 480s) — possibly 'unlimited' if MPS reports >24 GB, so pin ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH=acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B rather than relying on auto-pick. On macOS+MPS, get_gpu_config() force-overrides regardless of tier: lm_backend_restriction=pt_mlx_only (vllm requires CUDA), recommended_backend=mlx, offload_to_cpu_default=False and offload_dit_to_cpu_default=False (unified memory makes offload pointless), quantization_default=False (torchao unsupported on MPS), compile_model_default=False (torch.compile unsupported; if compile requested it is redirected to mx.compile for MLX components only). PyTorch model dtype on MPS is float32 (only cuda gets bf16/fp16, xpu bf16). The auto-offload heuristic in startup_model_init ('GPU < 20GB -> offload_to_cpu') CAN re-enable offload on a Mac reporting <20 GB unless overridden — set ACESTEP_OFFLOAD_TO_CPU=false explicitly on macOS; with ~24 GB reported on a 32 GB M2 Max it stays off. DiT+1.7B LM total weights ≈ 4.7+3.4+1.2+0.33 ≈ 9.6 GB, fitting easily in 32 GB unified memory; the 4B LM (8 GB) also fits if desired.

evidence.quote: elif hasattr(torch.backends, "mps") ... mps_module.recommended_max_memory() ... # MPS can use up to ~75% of unified memory ... memory_gb = (total_system_bytes / (1024**3)) * 0.75

On macOS with MPS ... compile_model_default = False ... quantization_default = False ... recommended_backend = "mlx" ... lm_backend_restriction = "pt_mlx_only" ... offload_to_cpu_default = False ... mlx_vae_chunk_size=_auto_mlx_vae_chunk_size(gpu_memory_gb) if _mps else 512
elif resolved_device == "cuda": ... torch.bfloat16 ... else: self.dtype = torch.bfloat16 if resolved_device == "xpu" else torch.float32
auto_offload = gpu_memory_gb > 0 and gpu_memory_gb < VRAM_AUTO_OFFLOAD_THRESHOLD_GB ... offload_to_cpu_env = os.getenv("ACESTEP_OFFLOAD_TO_CPU") ... else: offload_to_cpu = auto_offload
"tier6b": { # 20-24GB ... "max_batch_size_with_lm": 8, ... "recommended_lm_model": "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B", ... "offload_to_cpu_default": False, "quantization_default": False }

claims.claim: Three MLX components, all enabled automatically when device is mps (or cpu) on Apple Silicon: (1) MLX DiT — initialize_service(use_mlx_dit=True default) converts the loaded PyTorch DiT into a native MLXDiTDecoder (MLXDiTDecoder.from_config + convert_and_load); falls back to PyTorch silently on failure; status appears in the init message ('MLX DiT: Active (native MLX)'). (2) MLX VAE — always attempted on mps/cpu; decode chunk size auto-tuned by unified memory (<=16GB->256, <=36GB->512, <=64GB->1024, else 2048; min 192), override with ACESTEP_MLX_VAE_CHUNK; optional fp16 conversion via ACESTEP_MLX_VAE_FP16=1 (default fp32). (3) MLX LM backend — ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND=mlx (set by start_api_server_macos.sh) loads the 5Hz LM via mlx_lm.utils.load (with a 'model.' weight-key prefix remap fallback for ACE-Step checkpoints); requested vllm on mps is auto-redirected to MLX; failure falls back to PyTorch. Also relevant: decoded wavs are offloaded to CPU on MPS when effective MPS memory <32 GB (override ACESTEP_MPS_DECODE_OFFLOAD).

evidence.quote: if use_mlx_dit and device in ("mps", "cpu"): mlx_ok = self._init_mlx_dit(...) ... mlx_vae_status = "Disabled"; if device in ("mps", "cpu"): mlx_vae_ok = self._init_mlx_vae()
mlx_decoder = MLXDiTDecoder.from_config(self.config); convert_and_load(self.model, mlx_decoder) ... self.use_mlx_dit = True
_auto_mlx_vae_chunk_size ... env_val = os.environ.get("ACESTEP_MLX_VAE_CHUNK") ... if mem_gb <= 16: size = 256 elif mem_gb <= 36: size = 512 elif mem_gb <= 64: size = 1024 else: size = 2048
if backend == "mlx" or (backend == "vllm" and device == "mps"): ... self._load_mlx_model(full_lm_model_path) ... from mlx_lm.utils import load as mlx_load ... retrying with 'model.' prefix remapping

conversion based on ``ACESTEP_MLX_VAE_FP16`` ... use_fp16 = os.environ.get("ACESTEP_MLX_VAE_FP16", "0").lower() in (

claims.claim: .env.example contains exactly these variables (api_server loads .env once with override=False, so real env vars win): ACTIVE — ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH=acestep-v15-turbo (DiT path); ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH=acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B (options 0.6B/1.7B/4B); ACESTEP_DEVICE=auto (auto/cuda/cpu/xpu; note: 'mps' is valid in code via device resolution — auto picks mps on Mac); ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND=vllm (vllm/pt — code also accepts mlx; on macOS set mlx, vllm is impossible on MPS); ACESTEP_INIT_LLM=auto (auto/true/false; false disables thinking mode, CoT, sample mode, format mode). COMMENTED — ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR (shared model dir); ACESTEP_DOWNLOAD_SOURCE=auto (huggingface/modelscope); ACESTEP_API_KEY (sk-...); PORT=7860 (Gradio only, NOT the API server); SERVER_NAME=127.0.0.1 (Gradio only); LANGUAGE=en (Gradio only); ACESTEP_BATCH_SIZE=2 (Gradio default batch); ACESTEP_NO_INIT (comment: 'By default models are lazy-loaded on first request... Set to false to force eager model loading'). macOS-relevant: ACESTEP_DEVICE=auto resolves to mps; ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND must be mlx or pt; ACESTEP_INIT_LLM, ACESTEP_NO_INIT, ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR all apply. PORT/SERVER_NAME/LANGUAGE/ACESTEP_BATCH_SIZE are irrelevant to the REST API server (it uses ACESTEP_API_HOST/ACESTEP_API_PORT instead).

evidence.quote: ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH=acestep-v15-turbo ... ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH=acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B ... ACESTEP_DEVICE=auto ... ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND=vllm ... ACESTEP_INIT_LLM=auto ... # ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR= ... # ACESTEP_DOWNLOAD_SOURCE=auto ... # ACESTEP_API_KEY=sk-your-secret-key ... # PORT=7860 ... # SERVER_NAME=127.0.0.1 ... # LANGUAGE=en ... # ACESTEP_BATCH_SIZE=2 ... # ACESTEP_NO_INIT=true
_load_project_env ... load_dotenv(env_path, override=False)
VALID_LM_BACKENDS = {"vllm", "pt", "mlx"}

claims.topic: (f) api_server CLI flags — complete list with defaults
claims.claim: acestep-api (acestep.api_server:main -> run_api_server_main) accepts exactly 7 flags: --host (default env ACESTEP_API_HOST or 127.0.0.1); --port (int, default env ACESTEP_API_PORT or 8001); --api-key (default env ACESTEP_API_KEY); --download-source (choices huggingface/modelscope/auto, default env ACESTEP_DOWNLOAD_SOURCE or auto); --init-llm (store_true; default from env_bool(ACESTEP_INIT_LLM, False) — sets ACESTEP_INIT_LLM=true when given); --lm-model-path (default env ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH or ""); --no-init (store_true; default env_bool(ACESTEP_NO_INIT, False) — sets ACESTEP_NO_INIT=true). It then runs uvicorn 'acestep.api_server:app' with reload=False, workers=1 (workers MUST stay 1: in-memory queue/job store). Note asymmetry: passing --no-init sets the env var, but NOT passing it leaves the env untouched and the runtime default in startup_model_init is lazy=True anyway; to force eager loading you must set ACESTEP_NO_INIT=false in env/.env (there is no --init CLI flag). Additional server tunables read only from env: ACESTEP_QUEUE_MAXSIZE=200, ACESTEP_QUEUE_WORKERS=1, ACESTEP_AVG_JOB_SECONDS=5.0, ACESTEP_AVG_WINDOW=50, ACESTEP_API_WORKERS=1 (executor threads), ACESTEP_TMPDIR, ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH2/ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH3 (optional second/third DiT handler slots), ACESTEP_USE_FLASH_ATTENTION (default true; harmless no-op on macOS since flash-attn requires CUDA), ACESTEP_OFFLOAD_TO_CPU / ACESTEP_OFFLOAD_DIT_TO_CPU / ACESTEP_COMPILE_MODEL (default false), ACESTEP_VAE_CHECKPOINT, ACESTEP_GENERATION_TIMEOUT=600.

evidence.quote: --host default=os.getenv("ACESTEP_API_HOST", "127.0.0.1") ... --port default=int(os.getenv("ACESTEP_API_PORT", "8001")) ... --api-key ... --download-source choices=["huggingface", "modelscope", "auto"] ... --init-llm ... --lm-model-path ... --no-init ... uvicorn.run("acestep.api_server:app", ..., reload=False, workers=1)

QUEUE_MAXSIZE = int(os.getenv("ACESTEP_QUEUE_MAXSIZE", "200")); WORKER_COUNT = int(os.getenv("ACESTEP_QUEUE_WORKERS", "1")); INITIAL_AVG_JOB_SECONDS = float(os.getenv("ACESTEP_AVG_JOB_SECONDS", "5.0")); AVG_WINDOW = int(os.getenv("ACESTEP_AVG_WINDOW", "50"))
config_path2 = os.getenv("ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH2", "").strip(); config_path3 = os.getenv("ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH3", "").strip() ... max_workers = int(os.getenv("ACESTEP_API_WORKERS", "1"))

config_path = os.getenv("ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH", "acestep-v15-turbo"); device = os.getenv("ACESTEP_DEVICE", "auto"); use_flash_attention = env_bool("ACESTEP_USE_FLASH_ATTENTION", True) ... offload_dit_to_cpu = env_bool("ACESTEP_OFFLOAD_DIT_TO_CPU", False); compile_model = env_bool("ACESTEP_COMPILE_MODEL", False)

claims.topic: (g) Startup update-check behavior and disabling it
claims.claim: The update check is purely shell-script-level; the Python server (acestep-api / uvicorn) performs NO update check. start_api_server_macos.sh hardcodes CHECK_UPDATE="true" (line 43) and _startup_update_check() (lines 69-131) runs `git fetch origin` with a 10s timeout (gtimeout/timeout/no-timeout fallback), compares HEAD to origin/<branch>, and if they differ BLOCKS on an interactive `read -rp "Update now before starting? (Y/N)"` prompt; choosing Y runs check_update.sh which does `git reset --hard origin/<branch>` (with stash/backup logic) — this would silently move you off the pinned v0.1.8 tag. Because CHECK_UPDATE is a plain assignment (not :=default), exporting CHECK_UPDATE=false does NOT override it for the API launcher (it does work for start_gradio_ui_macos.sh, which uses `: "${CHECK_UPDATE:=true}"`). For unattended runs: (1) preferred — bypass the launcher entirely and run `uv run acestep-api --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8001` (or `.venv/bin/acestep-api`), which also skips the launcher's embedded-Python/MLX pip-fix logic; or (2) edit line 43 to CHECK_UPDATE="false" (note: tracked file, the edit is lost on git update). Also note: on a detached-HEAD tag checkout, `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` returns 'HEAD' and origin/HEAD usually resolves to the default branch, so the prompt is likely to trigger — another reason to bypass the launcher.

evidence.quote:
# Update check on startup (set to "false" to disable)
CHECK_UPDATE="true" ... _startup_update_check() { [[ "$CHECK_UPDATE" != "true" ]] && return 0 ... gtimeout 10 git fetch origin --quiet ... read -rp "Update now before starting? (Y/N): " update_choice ... bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/check_update.sh"
read -rp "Do you want to update now? (Y/N): " UPDATE_CHOICE ... if "$GIT_PATH" reset --hard "origin/$CURRENT_BRANCH" &>/dev/null; then
: "${CHECK_UPDATE:=true}"  # env-overridable default, unlike the API launcher
CHECK_UPDATE="true" ... [[ "$CHECK_UPDATE" != "true" ]] && return 0  # Linux launcher has identical hardcoded behavior

claims.topic: Gotcha: ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR is inconsistently honored by the API server
claims.claim: Two download code paths coexist. The api-server's pre-download step (startup_model_init.do_model_initialization) ALWAYS uses <repo_root>/checkpoints (os.path.join(project_root, "checkpoints")) and ignores ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR; the model inventory in /health//v1/models likewise scans <repo_root>/checkpoints. But handler.initialize_service and the acestep-download CLI DO honor ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR (and ACESTEP_PROJECT_ROOT). Setting ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR with the API server therefore risks double downloads (server downloads to repo/checkpoints, handler downloads/loads from the env dir) and inventory mismatches. For the WAVESMITH stack, the safe choice is to NOT set ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR and let everything live in <repo>/checkpoints, pre-seeded by running acestep-download FROM the repo root.

evidence.quote: checkpoint_dir = os.path.join(project_root, "checkpoints") ... ensure_model_downloaded(dit_model_name, checkpoint_dir)
env_ckpt = os.environ.get("ACESTEP_CHECKPOINTS_DIR"); if env_ckpt: checkpoint_dir = str(get_checkpoints_dir()) elif project_root: checkpoint_dir = os.path.join(project_root, "checkpoints")
project_root = get_project_root(); checkpoint_dir = os.path.join(project_root, "checkpoints")

claims.topic: LLM init decision (ACESTEP_INIT_LLM) and what disabling costs
claims.claim: LLM initialization at model-init time: ACESTEP_INIT_LLM empty/'auto' -> use gpu_config.init_lm_default (true for tier3+ i.e. >6GB; true on a 32GB Mac); 'true/1/yes/y/on' -> force enable; anything else -> force disable. LM model selection: ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH if set, else tier-recommended (1.7B on tier5/6a/6b, 4B on unlimited), with size-class support validation and fallback. Backend resolved via resolve_lm_backend (on MPS vllm is rewritten to mlx/pt). When disabled, app.state._llm_lazy_load_disabled=true and the .env documents the lost features: thinking mode, CoT caption/language detection, sample mode (/create_random_sample), format mode (/format_input). LM device/offload overridable via ACESTEP_LM_DEVICE and ACESTEP_LM_OFFLOAD_TO_CPU.

evidence.quote: init_llm_env = os.getenv("ACESTEP_INIT_LLM", "").strip().lower(); init_llm = gpu_config.init_lm_default ... elif init_llm_env in {"1", "true", "yes", "y", "on"} ... lm_backend = resolve_lm_backend(os.getenv("ACESTEP_LM_BACKEND"), gpu_config); lm_device = os.getenv("ACESTEP_LM_DEVICE", device) ... app.state._llm_lazy_load_disabled = True

When LLM is disabled, these features are unavailable: - Thinking mode (thinking=true) - Chain-of-Thought caption/language detection - Sample mode (generate from description) - Format mode (LLM-enhanced input)

claims.topic: On-demand model switching via API (relevant for the TS client)
claims.claim: POST /v1/init {model?, slot? (1-3), init_llm?, lm_model_path?} initializes or switches DiT/LM models at runtime, downloading missing checkpoints, and runs in a thread executor under app.state._init_lock (server stays responsive — unlike the lazy first-request path). Slots 2/3 only exist if ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH2/ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH3 were set at startup. POST /v1/reinitialize also exists for reloading with new settings. This is the recommended pre-warm call for the Next.js app after server start: POST /v1/init with empty body initializes defaults, then poll /health for models_initialized=true.

@app.post("/v1/reinitialize")

open_questions: Exact on-disk download size of ACE-Step/Ace-Step1.5: the repo's own docs say ~10 GB (docs/en/INSTALL.md:33) and bf16 weight constants sum to ~9.6 GB, but actual HF repo file sizes (incl. tokenizer/config extras) were not verified network-side per the read-only/no-network constraint.

What torch.mps.recommended_max_memory() reports on this specific 32 GB M2 Max (determines tier6b vs unlimited and therefore auto-picked LM 1.7B vs 4B) — verify at M0 runtime with MAX_MPS_VRAM unset; pinning ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH=acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B sidesteps it.

Whether the checkpoint config.json shipped in HF repo acestep-v15-sft sets is_turbo=false (expected): if so, GET /v1/models will report extract/lego/complete for sft models even though README/UI say sft does not support them — the TS client should encode the stricter pure-base rule rather than trusting supported_task_types.
The pinned v0.1.8 tag could not be confirmed via git metadata in this session (pyproject version reads 1.5.0; .git exists but no git commands were run beyond file inspection).

verdicts.correction: All mechanics confirmed, but the final prediction is wrong for THIS clone: .git/config restricts the fetch refspec to the v0.1.8 tag only, so `git fetch origin` creates no origin/* branch refs; `git rev-parse origin/HEAD` fails, remote_commit is empty, and _startup_update_check takes the 'Already up to date' early-return (start_api_server_macos.sh:98-104) — the interactive prompt will NOT trigger. The reset --hard danger only applies to a normally-cloned repo. Bypassing the launcher remains good practice (it also skips the embedded-Python/MLX pip-fix logic at lines 143-195), and the pin v0.1.8 IS verifiable from .git metadata, resolving the analyst's open question.

verdicts.correction: Mechanics confirmed, but the pre-warm recommendation needs a caveat: init_llm defaults to False, so an empty-body /v1/init initializes ONLY the DiT/VAE/text-encoder — the LM stays unloaded (llm_initialized=false) and, because /v1/init sets _initialized=True, the startup path that would auto-select the tier-recommended LM is skipped; the LM then loads lazily on the first generation request needing it (acestep/api/llm_readiness.py) with default acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B — NOT 1.7B — unless ACESTEP_LM_MODEL_PATH is set or the request passes lm_model_path. To pre-warm fully on the Mac: POST /v1/init {"init_llm": true, "lm_model_path": "acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B"} (note this also mutates process env at model_init_service.py:135-136).

missed: Pin verification was possible without running git: .git/HEAD contains dce621408bee8c31b4fcf4811682eb9359e1bc94 which equals refs/tags/v0.1.8 in .git/packed-refs, and .git/config's fetch refspec is tag-only (+refs/tags/v0.1.8:refs/tags/v0.1.8). This both confirms the v0.1.8 pin (analyst open question 4) and neutralizes the launcher update-check prompt risk for this clone.

CORS policy matters for the Next.js app: route_setup.py:62-68 restricts browser origins to 'null', http://localhost, http://127.0.0.1 plus regex ^https?://(localhost|127.0.0.1)(:port)?$, methods GET/POST/OPTIONS, headers Content-Type/Authorization. Browser-side fetches from any non-localhost origin will fail CORS — call the engine from the Next.js server side (route handlers), which is unaffected.

The API server lifespan DELETES proxy env vars (http_proxy/https_proxy/HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/ALL_PROXY) at startup (acestep/api/lifespan_runtime.py:56-57), so model downloads from inside the API server ignore any system proxy; users behind proxies must pre-download via the CLI.

/health's loaded_model is misleading: it is inventory default_model = basename(ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH) (model_service_routes.py:64,105,121,158) and is non-null even before any model loads. A TS client must key readiness ONLY on models_initialized (and llm_initialized for LM features).

There IS per-request LLM lazy loading: ensure_llm_ready_for_request (acestep/api/llm_readiness.py:9-88, invoked from job_blocking_generation.py:82) initializes the LM on demand during generation unless _llm_lazy_load_disabled or a prior _llm_init_error; once an error/block is recorded it is never retried (llm_readiness.py:36), and CoT features are silently auto-disabled for those requests. Its defaults differ from startup: lm default acestep-5Hz-lm-0.6B and backend default 'vllm' without resolve_lm_backend (lines 62-66) — on MPS this still lands on MLX only because llm_inference.py:678 redirects vllm+mps to MLX.

The API server's MODEL_REPO_MAPPING (acestep/api/model_download.py:8-18) omits acestep-v15-turbo-shift1, acestep-v15-turbo-continuous, and all three XL models; unknown names fall back to the unified repo. Downloads for those DiTs still work because handler.initialize_service runs its own _ensure_models_present → model_downloader.ensure_dit_model with the full SUBMODEL_REGISTRY (acestep/core/generation/handler/init_service_downloads.py:31-48); the handler path also auto-downloads the main model and non-official VAE variants.

Two different 'already downloaded' checks: the API-server pre-download treats any non-empty directory as present (api/model_download.py:100 os.path.exists + os.listdir), while the CLI/handler require an actual weights file (_contains_model_weights, model_downloader.py:362-383). A partially-downloaded folder can fool the API-level check.

Misleading startup log: do_model_initialization prints 'Auto-enabling CPU offload (GPU < 16GB)' / 'disabled by default (GPU >= 16GB)' (startup_model_init.py:41-44) but the real threshold constant is VRAM_AUTO_OFFLOAD_THRESHOLD_GB = 20.0 (gpu_config.py:36) — don't trust the log text when documenting behavior.

progress_text is a single GLOBAL last-log-line buffer for the whole process (one LogBuffer installed at module import, api_server.py:183-184); with concurrent jobs the progress_text returned for one task_id can be a log line from another job. STATUS_MAP maps queued and running both to 0 (server_utils.py:10), succeeded=1, failed=2 (unknown statuses also map to 2).

Full REST surface also includes POST /v1/create_sample (sample_format_routes.py:162) alongside /create_random_sample and /format_input; note /release_task, /query_result, /create_random_sample and /format_input authenticate via verify_token_from_request (token in request) rather than the Depends(verify_api_key) header dependency used by /v1/* routes — the TS client should send auth in the form each route expects.

Per-generation watchdog: ACESTEP_GENERATION_TIMEOUT defaults to 600s (acestep/core/generation/handler/generate_music_execute.py:11-12; same default in openrouter_adapter.py:46) — long renders on MPS may need this raised, and the TS client's polling budget should exceed it.

MPS memory detection has two more fallbacks the analyst skipped: torch.mps.get_device_properties(0).total_memory before the sysctl*0.75 path, and a final conservative 8.0 GB constant if all detection fails (gpu_config.py:598-628); MAX_MPS_VRAM env can simulate/override for testing (gpu_config.py:25,537-548).
ACESTEP_SAVE_MEMORY=1 enables save_memory_mode (skip intermediate tensors, disable auto_lrc/auto_score) (gpu_config.py:27,260-263) — a useful knob on a 32GB unified-memory Mac.

Job-store retention parameters relevant to the client: completed jobs are cleaned after 24h (JOB_STORE_MAX_AGE_SECONDS=86400, cleanup every 300s) and cached results expire after 7 days; an unknown/expired task_id in /query_result returns {result: "[]", status: 0} — indistinguishable from 'queued' (api_server.py:109-113; query_result_service.py:195), so the TS client should treat persistent status-0 with empty result as a possible expiry.
